title: R vs K Selection Nuance
script_summary: Exploring the subtle differences in r vs K selection in biology.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you that not all species fit neatly into r or K categories?
In ecology, r and K selection describe life strategies. R strategists reproduce quickly, while K strategists invest in fewer offspring.
But there's more! Not all species strictly adhere to these labels. Some blend traits from both strategies.
Consider species like turtles and elephants; they show characteristics of both r and K selection, defying the simple definitions.
Understanding these nuances helps us grasp the complexity of life. It’s not black and white; nature is a spectrum.
Curious about more biology insights? Hit that follow for more science mysteries!
